description_long: This object consists of three blue banners, each mounted on a vertical black pole. The banners are rectangular and appear to be made of fabric, with realistic folds and waves suggesting they are fluttering in the wind. The leftmost banner displays the phrase 'Let there be peace' in white text, the center banner features a large white peace symbol, and the rightmost banner has Cyrillic text reading 'Я за мир.' The banners are visually unified by their blue color and anti-war messaging. The poles are simple, cylindrical, and likely made of metal, providing sturdy support for the banners. The banners are designed for outdoor display, suitable for public demonstrations, parks, or urban streets.
description: A set of three blue fabric banners on black metal poles, each displaying peace-themed text or symbols. The banners are slightly wavy, with clear anti-war messages in English, Cyrillic, and a peace sign.
description_view_0: Three blue banners on black poles, with visible text and a peace symbol facing forward.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the banners and poles, with minimal text or symbols visible.
description_view_2: Rear view of the banners, with text and symbols mirrored and less legible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the banners and poles.
